style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;endArrow=none;startFill=0;strokeColor=default;dashed=1;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-5" target="tID9ocgDNQgydVk7ZSjG-39"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-5" value="They see their roles that they have for abs-abstrauth, because, well, why shouldn't they. The roles belong to them!" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="680" y="-500" width="190" height="90" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-7" value="Which accounts should which users be able to see? Is it related to all those in the current org? Or is it that if you are an account manager, you can see all accounts? No that ain't right because I don't want Joe Bloggs to see all my abstratium employees!" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="890" y="-500" width="320" height="130" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-8" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;endArrow=none;startFill=1;strokeColor=default;dashed=1;startArrow=classic;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-7" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-5"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="690" y="-443" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="610" y="-442" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-9" value="This too is a functional question and here are the specs:<div><ul><li>ADMIN can see everyone. that's why no one gets it.</li><li>An org owner can see all the accounts that are members in the org which they signed into.</li><li>An org member can see other accounts in the org too.</li><li>The role "abstratium-abstrauth_manage-accounts' is given to all initial org owners, but they can only manage accounts that have membership to their org.</li><li>An org owner can add anyone to their org</li><ul><li>But then we have a security issue, since Joe Bloggs can add abstratium.dev@gmail.com to their org, and they would be able to manage my roles in abstrauth!, although only within their organisation. So if I signed in and selected their org I might have different roles than when I sign in with the org abstratium.</li><li>Equally, Joe Bloggs must be able to give anyone the role "abstratium-abstratuth_manage-accounts" within their org.</li><li>The solution is that AccountRole is stored <i>per organisation</i>. When a user signs in, they are given the roles based only on the org that they chose, The selection is done by orgId, clientId and accountId.</li></ul></ul></div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=left;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="890" y="-345" width="910" height="215" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-32" value="Joe Bloggs' "manage clients" abstrauth role only allows him to manage clients <i>within his org!</i>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-33" value="Joe Bloggs' "manage accounts" abstrauth role only allows him to manage account membership and roles&nbsp;<i>within his org!</i>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-34"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1473" y="280" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="1467" y="380" as="targetPoint" /> <Array as="points"> <mxPoint x="1220" y="260" /> </Array> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-36" value="user<div>role a</div><div>role b</div>" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1490" y="248" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="1661" y="287" as="targetPoint" /> <Array as="points"> <mxPoint x="1270" y="360" /> </Array> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-37" value="Roles like these are used in @RolesAllowed in his application." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-38" value="<div>&lt;&lt;application&gt;&gt;</div>asdf-someapp" style="shape=module;align=left;spacingLeft=20;align=center;verticalAlign=top;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#f5f5f5;fontColor=#333333;strokeColor=#666666;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1110" y="600" width="100" height="50" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-39" value="authenticates<div>with and</div><div>uses roles<span style="background-color: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">&nbsp;in</span></div><div>@RolesAllowed</div>" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-38" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-34"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1564" y="580" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="1690" y="348" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-40" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;dashed=1;endArrow=none;startFill=0;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-37"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1310" y="370" as="targetPoint" /> <mxPoint x="1636" y="160" as="sourcePoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-41" value="Applications cannot authenticate with foreign clients, since they don't know the client secrets!!" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1250" y="540" width="190" height="60" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-42" value="So which accounts does he see? All those in his org, since he is an account manager in his org." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1180" y="-30" width="190" height="80" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-43" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;dashed=1;endArrow=none;startFill=1;startArrow=classic;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-42" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-33"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1570" y="220" as="targetPoint" /> <mxPoint x="1636" y="160" as="sourcePoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-44" value="How does Joe add more account managers for his org? He has to be able to manage the roles on the abstrauth client! well he can see the accounts, and then add a role. The ABAC check needs to be: if client is subscribed to (and so public) and user has "manage clients" role, then they can add the role. It is added using the orgId from the users JWT and is loaded whenever that user signs in." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1385" y="-120" width="310" height="140" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-45" value="Can this be abused? Can such a user then add themselves as admin to abstrauth?&nbsp;No they can't because only the roles in the roles allowed list can be used and admin isn't in there, preceisely because the role isn't self-administerable." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1770" y="-80" width="310" height="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-46" value=""Joe might be an account manager, but he can only manage accounts that are in his org."<div><br></div><div>Ah wait! So we need an AccountRole per Org so that if my account is also in his org, i can be a non-manager there, but a manager in mine? probably!</div><div><br></div><div>The first org owner is an account manager by default. They can add that role to other org owners that they add.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>When a user registers, always give them the user role to abstrauth in the org of the user who is adding them.&nbsp;</div><div>When the token is created, only read for the orgId for which the token is being created.</div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="1920" y="40" width="200" height="360" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-47" value="Which OAuth Clients should a user see? This is a functional question and here are the specs:<div><ul><li>all clients that have the orgId of the org into which the user signed in,</li><li>all clients to which the users org is also subscribed.</li></ul></div>" style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=left;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="870" y="-690" width="350" height="130" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-48" style="rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;endArrow=none;startFill=1;strokeColor=default;dashed=1;startArrow=classic;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-47" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-5"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="690" y="-443" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="610" y="-442" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-49" value="<div style=""><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">public client with auto-subscribe = true</span></div>" style="ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;align=center;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="390" y="-240" width="140" height="70" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-50" value="<div style=""><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">public client with auto-subscribe = false</span></div>" style="ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;align=center;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="480" y="-153" width="140" height="70" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-51" value="once the first user signs in to this client, the subscription is created and the user gets the default roles from the allowed roles table. Subsequent users also get them." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="560" y="-257.5" width="200" height="97.5" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-52" value="Such clients are available to the asdf org owner to select for subscription. Then once a user signs into it, they get the default roles. Owners can modify roles based on the allowed roles list." style="shape=note2;boundedLbl=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;size=11;verticalAlign=top;align=center;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" vertex="1" parent="1"> <mxGeometry x="630" y="-158" width="190" height="118" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-53" value="" style="endArrow=none;dashed=1;html=1;rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-49" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-51"> <mxGeometry width="50" height="50" relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="500" y="-303" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="530" y="-304" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-54" value="" style="endArrow=none;dashed=1;html=1;rounded=0;sketch=1;hachureGap=4;jiggle=2;curveFitting=1;fontFamily=Architects Daughter;fontSource=https%3A%2F%2Ffonts.googleapis.com%2Fcss%3Ffamily%3DArchitects%2BDaughter;" edge="1" parent="1" source="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-50" target="9T5_VDhA3Tx296kR6vuW-52"> <mxGeometry width="50" height="50" relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="540" y="-196" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="570" y="-197" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

div.div: Which accounts should which users be able to see? Is it related to all those in the current org? Or is it that if you are an account manager, you can see all accounts? No that ain't right because I don't want Joe Bloggs to see all my abstratium employees!

switch.text: Which accounts should which users be able to see? Is...

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 908px; height: 1px; padding-top: 363px; margin-left: 1112px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: "Architects Daughter"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
This too is a functional question and here are the specs:
ADMIN can see everyone. that's why no one gets it.
An org owner can see all the accounts that are members in the org which they signed into.
An org member can see other accounts in the org too.
The role "abstratium-abstrauth_manage-accounts' is given to all initial org owners, but they can only manage accounts that have membership to their org.
An org owner can add anyone to their org
But then we have a security issue, since Joe Bloggs can add abstratium.dev@gmail.com to their org, and they would be able to manage my roles in abstrauth!, although only within their organisation. So if I signed in and selected their org I might have different roles than when I sign in with the org abstratium.
Equally, Joe Bloggs must be able to give anyone the role "abstratium-abstratuth_manage-accounts" within their org.
The solution is that AccountRole is stored per organisation. When a user signs in, they are given the roles based only on the org that they chose, The selection is done by orgId, clientId and accountId.

switch.text: This too is a functional question and here are the specs:...

div.div: How does Joe add more account managers for his org? He has to be able to manage the roles on the abstrauth client! well he can see the accounts, and then add a role. The ABAC check needs to be: if client is subscribed to (and so public) and user has "manage clients" role, then they can add the role. It is added using the orgId from the users JWT and is loaded whenever that user signs in.

switch.text: How does Joe add more account managers for his org?...

div.div: Can this be abused? Can such a user then add themselves as admin to abstrauth? No they can't because only the roles in the roles allowed list can be used and admin isn't in there, preceisely because the role isn't self-administerable.

div.div:
"Joe might be an account manager, but he can only manage accounts that are in his org."
Ah wait! So we need an AccountRole per Org so that if my account is also in his org, i can be a non-manager there, but a manager in mine? probably!
The first org owner is an account manager by default. They can add that role to other org owners that they add.
When a user registers, always give them the user role to abstrauth in the org of the user who is adding them.
When the token is created, only read for the orgId for which the token is being created.

div.div:
Which OAuth Clients should a user see? This is a functional question and here are the specs:
all clients that have the orgId of the org into which the user signed in,
all clients to which the users org is also subscribed.
